compiled_state.title: Deep tier sonnet review agents ignore FINDINGS_OUTPUT — write to canonical path instead of slot files
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Deep tier review (REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4) dispatches 3 parallel sonnet agents with FINDINGS_OUTPUT set to slot-specific paths (reviewer-findings-{a,b,c}.json). All 3 agents ignored FINDINGS_OUTPUT and wrote to the canonical reviewer-findings.json, causing a last-writer-wins race. The opus arch reviewer received findings from only 1 of 3 agents.

Observed during merge commit review in worktree-20260324-091358. The named agents (dso:code-reviewer-deep-correctness, dso:code-reviewer-deep-verification, dso:code-reviewer-deep-hygiene) use write-reviewer-findings.sh which writes to the canonical path by default. The --output flag exists but the agents' system prompts may not instruct them to use FINDINGS_OUTPUT to set the output path.

Root cause likely in the agent definitions at plugins/dso/agents/ — they need to pass FINDINGS_OUTPUT to write-reviewer-findings.sh --output.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Root cause: write-reviewer-findings.sh hardcodes output to reviewer-findings.json (line 36). No --output flag exists. Agent definitions also hardcode the write path. When 3 parallel sonnet agents run in deep tier, they all clobber the same file. Fix: add --output flag to write-reviewer-findings.sh and update agent definitions to use FINDINGS_OUTPUT when provided.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
